title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement selkamand@ccia.org.au. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

what: Directly contribute to the ggoncoplot codebase
title: NA
text: welcome contributions community enhance expand functionality ggoncoplot. Whether want fix bug, add new features, improve documentation, optimize performance, efforts highly valued. get started: Fork Repository: Click ‘Fork’ button top right page create copy repository GitHub account. Clone Repository: Use git clone clone forked repository local machine. Create Branch Make Changes: Implement changes new branch Test Package: Run devtools::test() devtools::check() ensure changes break behaviours. Add additional unit tests cover new behaviour. Commit Push: Commit changes push branch forked repository. Create Pull Request: Go original repository open pull request branch. Please provide clear description changes relevant issues discussions.
code: git clone https://github.com/selkamand/ggoncoplot.git git checkout -b feature-name

title: Essential Resources
text: ggoncoplot manuals hosted online rather bundled software, keep downloads fast avoiding large filesizes interactive graphics. Installation & Quick Start Comprehensive Manual Source Code Manuscript

title: Manual
text: See Installation & Quick Start.

title: Manual
text: input ggoncoplot data.frame/data.table/tibble 1 row per mutation cohort columns describing following: Gene Symbol Sample Identifier (optional) mutation type (optional) tooltip (character string: show mouse hover particular mutation) columns can order, named anything. define mapping input dataset columns required features call ggoncoplot.

title: Manual
text: tidyTCGA package provides public tabular cancer datasets. ggoncoplot flexible input data; can use MAF files tabular mutation-level datasets specifying columns sample identifiers gene names.

title: Manual
code: # TCGA GBM dataset from TCGAmuations package gbm_csv <- system.file(package='ggoncoplot', "testdata/GBM_tcgamutations_mc3_maf.csv.gz") gbm_df <- read.csv(file = gbm_csv, header=TRUE)  gbm_df |>    ggoncoplot(     col_genes = 'Hugo_Symbol',      col_samples = 'Tumor_Sample_Barcode'   )

title: Manual
text: Colour mutation specifying col_mutation_type
code: # TCGA GBM dataset from TCGAmuations package gbm_csv <- system.file(package='ggoncoplot', "testdata/GBM_tcgamutations_mc3_maf.csv.gz") gbm_df <- read.csv(file = gbm_csv, header=TRUE)  gbm_df |>    ggoncoplot(     col_genes = 'Hugo_Symbol',      col_samples = 'Tumor_Sample_Barcode',      col_mutation_type = 'Variant_Classification'   ) #>  #> ── Identify Class ── #>  #> ℹ Found 9 unique mutation types in input set #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid PAVE terms #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid SO terms #> ℹ 9/9 mutation types were valid MAF terms #> ✔ Mutation Types are described using valid MAF terms ... using MAF palete

title: Manual
text: Show 4 frequently mutated genes using topn argument
code: gbm_df |>    ggoncoplot(     col_genes = 'Hugo_Symbol',      col_samples = 'Tumor_Sample_Barcode',      col_mutation_type = 'Variant_Classification',      topn = 4   ) #>  #> ── Identify Class ── #>  #> ℹ Found 9 unique mutation types in input set #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid PAVE terms #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid SO terms #> ℹ 9/9 mutation types were valid MAF terms #> ✔ Mutation Types are described using valid MAF terms ... using MAF palete

title: Manual
text: Use genes_to_ignore argument filter specific genes, TTN MUC16. can use packages like somaticflags get lists genes might want filter .
code: gbm_df |>    ggoncoplot(     col_genes = 'Hugo_Symbol',      col_samples = 'Tumor_Sample_Barcode',      col_mutation_type = 'Variant_Classification',      topn = 10,     genes_to_ignore = c("TTN", "MUC16")   ) #>  #> ── Identify Class ── #>  #> ℹ Found 9 unique mutation types in input set #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid PAVE terms #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid SO terms #> ℹ 9/9 mutation types were valid MAF terms #> ✔ Mutation Types are described using valid MAF terms ... using MAF palete

title: Manual
text: lets show TP53 TERT
code: gbm_df |>    ggoncoplot(     col_genes = 'Hugo_Symbol',      col_samples = 'Tumor_Sample_Barcode',      col_mutation_type = 'Variant_Classification',      genes_to_include = c('TP53', 'TERT'),   ) #>  #> ── Identify Class ── #>  #> ℹ Found 9 unique mutation types in input set #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid PAVE terms #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid SO terms #> ℹ 9/9 mutation types were valid MAF terms #> ✔ Mutation Types are described using valid MAF terms ... using MAF palete

title: Manual
text: show_all_samples argument add samples don’t mutations selected genes plot. Note supply metadata table, default samples lacking mutations still shown. can include samples setting metadata_require_mutations = FALSE isn’t recommended unless ’re sure sample truly mutations dataframe.
code: gbm_df |>    ggoncoplot(     col_genes = 'Hugo_Symbol',      col_samples = 'Tumor_Sample_Barcode',      col_mutation_type = 'Variant_Classification',      show_all_samples = TRUE   ) #>  #> ── Identify Class ── #>  #> ℹ Found 9 unique mutation types in input set #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid PAVE terms #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid SO terms #> ℹ 9/9 mutation types were valid MAF terms #> ✔ Mutation Types are described using valid MAF terms ... using MAF palete

title: Manual
text:
Use col_tooltip argument indicate column input dataframe used custom tooltip. Note tooltips html, want insert break, just paste
. Similarly, want make text tooltip bold, try "text_to_bold<\b>". Note single sample multiple mutations gene, represented one tile oncoplot, tooltip mutation shown (newline delimited).
code: # Add a tooltip column to data.frame gbm_df[["tooltip"]] <- with(gbm_df, paste0(Chromosome, ":", Start_Position, " ", Reference_Allele, ">", Tumor_Seq_Allele2))  # Generate oncoplot gbm_df |>    ggoncoplot(     col_genes = 'Hugo_Symbol',      col_samples = 'Tumor_Sample_Barcode',      col_mutation_type = 'Variant_Classification',      col_tooltip = 'tooltip' # We'll specify a custom tooltip based on our new 'tooltip' column   ) #>  #> ── Identify Class ── #>  #> ℹ Found 9 unique mutation types in input set #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid PAVE terms #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid SO terms #> ℹ 9/9 mutation types were valid MAF terms #> ✔ Mutation Types are described using valid MAF terms ... using MAF palete

title: Manual
text: can also add pathway information oncoplot supplying simple 2-column data.frame. Currently default order pathways genes plot based order appearnce pathway data.frame. Future versions ggoncoplot support data-based sorting. genes missing oncoplot displayed ‘’ pathway bottom plot.
code: path_pathways <- system.file("testdata/GBM_tcgamutations_mc3.pathways.csv", package = "ggoncoplot") pathways_df <- read.csv(path_pathways)  gbm_df |>    ggoncoplot(     col_genes = 'Hugo_Symbol',      col_samples = 'Tumor_Sample_Barcode',      col_mutation_type = 'Variant_Classification',      pathway = pathways_df   ) #> Found pathway column: Pathway #> Warning: 7 unknown levels in `f`: Cytoskeleton structure, Nuclear envelope structure, #> Muscle structure, Endocytosis, Extracellular matrix, Ciliary function, and #> Neurotransmitter release #>  #> ── Identify Class ── #>  #> ℹ Found 9 unique mutation types in input set #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid PAVE terms #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid SO terms #> ℹ 9/9 mutation types were valid MAF terms #> ✔ Mutation Types are described using valid MAF terms ... using MAF palete

title: Manual
text: many samples mutations Gene (optionally coloured mutation type)
code: gbm_df |>    ggoncoplot(     col_genes = 'Hugo_Symbol',      col_samples = 'Tumor_Sample_Barcode',      col_mutation_type = 'Variant_Classification',      draw_gene_barplot = TRUE   ) #>  #> ── Identify Class ── #>  #> ℹ Found 9 unique mutation types in input set #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid PAVE terms #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid SO terms #> ℹ 9/9 mutation types were valid MAF terms #> ✔ Mutation Types are described using valid MAF terms ... using MAF palete

title: Manual
text: can use set draw_tmb_barplot = TRUE plot total number mutations (total mutational burden) sample. datasets, presence one hypermutator makes hard see less extreme trends, defualt mutational burden plotted log10 scale. can changed setting log10_transform_tmb = FALSE
code: gbm_df |>    ggoncoplot(     col_genes = 'Hugo_Symbol',      col_samples = 'Tumor_Sample_Barcode',      col_mutation_type = 'Variant_Classification',     draw_tmb_barplot = TRUE,      # options = ggoncoplot_options(log10_transform_tmb = FALSE)"   ) #>  #> ── Identify Class ── #>  #> ℹ Found 9 unique mutation types in input set #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid PAVE terms #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid SO terms #> ℹ 9/9 mutation types were valid MAF terms #> ✔ Mutation Types are described using valid MAF terms ... using MAF palete #> ! TMB plot: Refusing to colour plot since `log10_transform_tmb = TRUE`. #> This is because you cannot accurately plot stacked bars on a logarithmic scale

title: Manual
text: custom approach computing TMB, can replace default tmb plot supplying custom two three column dataframe. Column order doesn’t matter, must supply following columns Sample identifiers (column name must match col_samples) quantitative variable representing TMB / value want add barplot (Optional) categorical variable used colour TMB plot. example. gbm_tmb.csv file describes tmb calculated total number INDELs (variants ignored). Sometimes might want colour custom TMB bars based qualitative value. , can just add categorical column tmb_data. Now sample can multiple tmb values different types, stacked top make barplot. Note , log10_transform_tmb ignored axis never log transformed - since stacked barplots misleading logarithmic axes. Note may need supply tmb_palette describing types underneath count.
code: # Read in custom TMB data to a data.frame gbm_tmb <- read.csv(system.file("testdata/GBM_tmb.csv", package = "ggoncoplot")) head(gbm_tmb) #>   Tumor_Sample_Barcode tmb #> 1      TCGA-02-0003-01   0 #> 2      TCGA-02-0033-01   2 #> 3      TCGA-02-0047-01   3 #> 4      TCGA-02-0055-01   0 #> 5      TCGA-02-2466-01   8 #> 6      TCGA-02-2470-01   2  ## Plot oncoplot with custom tmb dataframe gbm_df |>   ggoncoplot(     col_genes = 'Hugo_Symbol',      col_samples = 'Tumor_Sample_Barcode',      col_mutation_type = 'Variant_Classification',     draw_tmb_barplot = TRUE,     tmb_data = gbm_tmb,          # With custom tmb calculations you often also want to turn off the inbuilt log10 transformation      # and show the title (based on tmb_data column name)     options = ggoncoplot_options(       log10_transform_tmb = FALSE,        show_ylab_title_tmb = TRUE     )   ) #>  #> ── Found custom TMB dataset ── #>  #> ℹ Sample column: Tumor_Sample_Barcode #> ℹ TMB value column: tmb #> ℹ TMB subtypes column: None specified (values are sample level #>  #> ── Identify Class ── #>  #> ℹ Found 9 unique mutation types in input set #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid PAVE terms #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid SO terms #> ℹ 9/9 mutation types were valid MAF terms #> ✔ Mutation Types are described using valid MAF terms ... using MAF palete # Read in data.frame describing indel TMB but broken up by DELs & INS (based on counts) gbm_tmb_with_types <- read.csv(system.file("testdata/GBM_tmb_with_types.csv", package="ggoncoplot")) head(gbm_tmb_with_types) #>   Tumor_Sample_Barcode Variant_Type Mutations #> 1      TCGA-02-0003-01          DEL         0 #> 2      TCGA-02-0003-01          INS         0 #> 3      TCGA-02-0033-01          DEL         1 #> 4      TCGA-02-0033-01          INS         1 #> 5      TCGA-02-0047-01          DEL         3 #> 6      TCGA-02-0047-01          INS         0   ## Plot oncoplot with custom tmb dataframe gbm_df |>   ggoncoplot(     col_genes = 'Hugo_Symbol',      col_samples = 'Tumor_Sample_Barcode',      col_mutation_type = 'Variant_Classification',     draw_tmb_barplot = TRUE,     tmb_data = gbm_tmb_with_types,     tmb_palette = c("DEL" = "black", INS = "red"),     # With custom tmb calculations you often also want to turn off the inbuilt log10 transformation      # and show the title (based on tmb_data column name)     options = ggoncoplot_options(       log10_transform_tmb = FALSE,        show_ylab_title_tmb = TRUE     )   ) #>  #> ── Found custom TMB dataset ── #>  #> ℹ Sample column: Tumor_Sample_Barcode #> ℹ TMB value column: Mutations #> ℹ TMB value column: Variant_Type #>  #> ── Identify Class ── #>  #> ℹ Found 9 unique mutation types in input set #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid PAVE terms #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid SO terms #> ℹ 9/9 mutation types were valid MAF terms #> ✔ Mutation Types are described using valid MAF terms ... using MAF palete

what: Add both TMB and Gene Barplots
title: Manual
text: Usually, ’ll want draw margin plots (tmb + gene).
code: gbm_df |>    ggoncoplot(     col_genes = 'Hugo_Symbol',      col_samples = 'Tumor_Sample_Barcode',      col_mutation_type = 'Variant_Classification',     draw_tmb_barplot = TRUE,      draw_gene = TRUE     # log10_transform_tmb = FALSE   ) #>  #> ── Identify Class ── #>  #> ℹ Found 9 unique mutation types in input set #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid PAVE terms #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid SO terms #> ℹ 9/9 mutation types were valid MAF terms #> ✔ Mutation Types are described using valid MAF terms ... using MAF palete #> ! TMB plot: Refusing to colour plot since `log10_transform_tmb = TRUE`. #> This is because you cannot accurately plot stacked bars on a logarithmic scale

title: Manual
code: gbm_clinical_csv <- system.file(  package = "ggoncoplot",  "testdata/GBM_tcgamutations_mc3_clinical.csv" )  gbm_clinical_df <- read.csv(file = gbm_clinical_csv, header = TRUE)  ggoncoplot(  gbm_df,  col_genes = "Hugo_Symbol",  col_samples = "Tumor_Sample_Barcode",  col_mutation_type = "Variant_Classification",  metadata = gbm_clinical_df,  cols_to_plot_metadata = c('gender', 'histological_type', 'prior_glioma', 'tumor_tissue_site'),   draw_tmb_barplot = TRUE,   draw_gene_barplot = TRUE,   show_all_samples = TRUE ) #> ℹ 2 samples with metadata have no mutations. Fitering these out #> ℹ To keep these samples, set `metadata_require_mutations = FALSE`. To view them in the oncoplot ensure you additionally set `show_all_samples = TRUE` #> → TCGA-06-0165-01 #> → TCGA-06-0167-01 #>  #> ── Identify Class ── #>  #> ℹ Found 9 unique mutation types in input set #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid PAVE terms #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid SO terms #> ℹ 9/9 mutation types were valid MAF terms #> ✔ Mutation Types are described using valid MAF terms ... using MAF palete #> ! TMB plot: Refusing to colour plot since `log10_transform_tmb = TRUE`. #> This is because you cannot accurately plot stacked bars on a logarithmic scale #>  #> ── Plotting Sample Metadata ──────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ── Sorting #> ℹ Sorting X axis by: Order of appearance #>  #> ── Generating Plot #> ℹ Found 4 plottable columns in data

title: Manual
text: Sometimes want clinical annotations oncoplot. can achieved setting metadata_position = "top"
code: ggoncoplot(  gbm_df,  col_genes = "Hugo_Symbol",  col_samples = "Tumor_Sample_Barcode",  col_mutation_type = "Variant_Classification",  metadata = gbm_clinical_df,  cols_to_plot_metadata = c('gender', 'histological_type', 'prior_glioma', 'tumor_tissue_site'),   draw_tmb_barplot = FALSE,   draw_gene_barplot = TRUE,   show_all_samples = TRUE,  options = ggoncoplot_options(metadata_position = "top") ) #> ℹ 2 samples with metadata have no mutations. Fitering these out #> ℹ To keep these samples, set `metadata_require_mutations = FALSE`. To view them in the oncoplot ensure you additionally set `show_all_samples = TRUE` #> → TCGA-06-0165-01 #> → TCGA-06-0167-01 #>  #> ── Identify Class ── #>  #> ℹ Found 9 unique mutation types in input set #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid PAVE terms #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid SO terms #> ℹ 9/9 mutation types were valid MAF terms #> ✔ Mutation Types are described using valid MAF terms ... using MAF palete #>  #> ── Plotting Sample Metadata ──────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ── Sorting #> ℹ Sorting X axis by: Order of appearance #>  #> ── Generating Plot #> ℹ Found 4 plottable columns in data

title: Manual
text: sort oncoplot clinical annotations specify columns metadata data.frame want sort using metadata_sort_cols argument. Specifying multiple columns (shown ) hierarchical/stratified sort (sorts first column, second column, etc). finer control sort performed, see metadata_sort_desc metadata_sort_by arguments.
code: ggoncoplot(  gbm_df,  col_genes = "Hugo_Symbol",  col_samples = "Tumor_Sample_Barcode",  col_mutation_type = "Variant_Classification",  metadata = gbm_clinical_df,  cols_to_plot_metadata = c('gender', 'histological_type', 'prior_glioma', 'tumor_tissue_site'),   draw_tmb_barplot = TRUE,   draw_gene_barplot = TRUE,   show_all_samples = TRUE,  metadata_sort_cols = c("gender", "histological_type") # Sort by gender, then histological type ) #> ℹ 2 samples with metadata have no mutations. Fitering these out #> ℹ To keep these samples, set `metadata_require_mutations = FALSE`. To view them in the oncoplot ensure you additionally set `show_all_samples = TRUE` #> → TCGA-06-0165-01 #> → TCGA-06-0167-01 #>  #> ── Identify Class ── #>  #> ℹ Found 9 unique mutation types in input set #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid PAVE terms #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid SO terms #> ℹ 9/9 mutation types were valid MAF terms #> ✔ Mutation Types are described using valid MAF terms ... using MAF palete #> ! TMB plot: Refusing to colour plot since `log10_transform_tmb = TRUE`. #> This is because you cannot accurately plot stacked bars on a logarithmic scale #>  #> ── Plotting Sample Metadata ──────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ── Sorting #> ℹ Sorting X axis by: Order of appearance #>  #> ── Generating Plot #> ℹ Found 4 plottable columns in data

title: Manual
text: default, clicking tile oncoplot copy sample ID clipboard. can customised using copy argument. example can choose copy tooltip gene instead.
code: gbm_df |>   ggoncoplot(    col_genes = "Hugo_Symbol",    col_samples = "Tumor_Sample_Barcode",    col_mutation_type = "Variant_Classification",    copy = 'gene' # see ?ggoncoplot for other valid values   ) #>  #> ── Identify Class ── #>  #> ℹ Found 9 unique mutation types in input set #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid PAVE terms #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid SO terms #> ℹ 9/9 mutation types were valid MAF terms #> ✔ Mutation Types are described using valid MAF terms ... using MAF palete

title: Manual
text: Want control look oncoplot? ggoncoplot takes options argument help control visual parameters. See ?ggoncoplot_options full list parameters.
code: gbm_df |>    ggoncoplot(     # Data     col_genes = 'Hugo_Symbol',      col_samples = 'Tumor_Sample_Barcode',      col_mutation_type = 'Variant_Classification',      draw_tmb_barplot = TRUE,      draw_gene_barplot = TRUE,          #pathway = pathways_df,     metadata = gbm_clinical_df,      cols_to_plot_metadata = c('gender', 'histological_type', 'prior_glioma', 'tumor_tissue_site'),          # Customise Visual Options     options = ggoncoplot_options(              # Interactive Plot Options       interactive_svg_width = 12,       interactive_svg_height = 6,        # Relative the ratio of marginal plot size to main tile plot (% of total plot height/width)       plotsize_gene_rel_width = 40, # Genebar plot takes 50% of plot width       plotsize_tmb_rel_height = 30,       plotsize_metadata_rel_height = 15,          # Axis Titles       xlab_title = "Glioblastoma Samples",       ylab_title = "Top 10 mutated genes",          # Fontsizes       fontsize_xlab = 40,       fontsize_ylab = 40,       fontsize_genes = 16,       fontsize_samples = 12,       fontsize_count = 14,       fontsize_tmb_title = 14,       fontsize_tmb_axis = 11,       fontsize_pathway = 16,          # Customise Tiles       tile_height = 1,       tile_width = 1,       colour_backround = "white",       colour_mutation_type_unspecified = "grey10",          # Show different elements       show_sample_ids = FALSE,       show_ylab_title = FALSE,       show_xlab_title = FALSE,       show_ylab_title_tmb = FALSE,       show_axis_gene = TRUE,       show_axis_tmb = TRUE,          # Gene Barplot Specific Options       show_genebar_labels = TRUE,       genebar_label_padding = 0.1,       genebar_only_pad_when_labels_shown = TRUE,       genebar_label_nudge = 3,       genebar_label_round = 0,        # Transformation and label scales       log10_transform_tmb = TRUE,       scientific_tmb = FALSE,          # Pathway Faceting Colours / Text       colour_pathway_text = "black",       colour_pathway_bg = "white",       colour_pathway_outline = "black",       pathway_text_angle = 0,          # Legend number of columns       ggoncoplot_guide_ncol = 1     ) ) #> ℹ 2 samples with metadata have no mutations. Fitering these out #> ℹ To keep these samples, set `metadata_require_mutations = FALSE`. To view them in the oncoplot ensure you additionally set `show_all_samples = TRUE` #> → TCGA-06-0165-01 #> → TCGA-06-0167-01 #>  #> ── Identify Class ── #>  #> ℹ Found 9 unique mutation types in input set #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid PAVE terms #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid SO terms #> ℹ 9/9 mutation types were valid MAF terms #> ✔ Mutation Types are described using valid MAF terms ... using MAF palete #> ! TMB plot: Refusing to colour plot since `log10_transform_tmb = TRUE`. #> This is because you cannot accurately plot stacked bars on a logarithmic scale #>  #> ── Plotting Sample Metadata ──────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ── Sorting #> ℹ Sorting X axis by: Order of appearance #>  #> ── Generating Plot #> ℹ Found 4 plottable columns in data

title: Manual
text: building oncoplot interactive=FALSE result patchwork object. lets customise plot standard patchwork styling. example adding title plot_annotation() applying theme subplots (tilegraph & gene barplot) & theme(...).
code: library(patchwork)  library(ggplot2)   # Create oncoplot patchwork oncoplot_patchwork <- gbm_df |>    ggoncoplot(     col_genes = 'Hugo_Symbol',      col_samples = 'Tumor_Sample_Barcode',     draw_gene_barplot = TRUE,     interactive = FALSE   )  # Customise the theme oncoplot_patchwork +   plot_annotation(title = 'My Glioblastoma Oncoplot') &    theme(text = element_text('mono', face = "bold"))

title: Manual

title: Manual
text: ggoncoplot outputs can combined packages create interactive multiplots. show co-explore mutation oncoplots, expression t-SNE’s & methylation UMAPs.

what: Install additional packages required to generate interactive multiplots
title: Manual
text: following example uses additional packages: express creating expression plots patchwork combining oncoplot, expression methylation plots single graphic ggiraph making combined plot interactive somaticflags excluding genes commonly mutated somatic tissues oncoplot. install packages run:

title: Manual
text: create oncoplot can interactively coexplored visualisations, set interactive = FALSE ggoncoplot returns ggplot object instead ggiraph. express package visualises TCGA methylation expression data (UMAPs t-SNEs. can use patchwork compose plots single visualisation make entire group interactive data linked across plots.
code: # Load required libraries library(express) # For Creating Expression Plots library(patchwork) # For combining oncoplot and expression plots library(ggiraph) # For making combined plot interactive library(somaticflags) # For excluding genes commonly mutated in somatic tissues  ## Create a Breast Cancer Oncoplot brca_df <- read.csv(system.file("testdata/BRCA_tcgamutations_mc3.csv.gz", package = "ggoncoplot"))  brca_clinical <- read.csv(system.file("testdata/BRCA_tcgamutations_mc3_clinical.csv.gz", package = "ggoncoplot"))  ggoncoplot <- brca_df |>   ggoncoplot(     col_genes = 'Gene',     col_samples = 'Sample',     col_mutation_type = 'MutationType',     topn = 10,      genes_to_ignore = somaticflags,     metadata = brca_clinical,     metadata_palette =       list(         Progesterone = c("Indeterminate" = "gray80", "Negative" = "black", "Positive" = "#DF536B", "[Not Evaluated]" = "grey90"),         Estrogen = c("Indeterminate" = "gray80", "Negative" = "black", "Positive" = "#DF536B", "[Not Evaluated]" = "grey90"),         HER2 = c("Indeterminate" = "gray80", Equivocal =  "grey80", "Negative" = "black", "Positive" = "#DF536B", "[Not Evaluated]" = "grey90"),         Classification = c("Ambiguous" = "gray80", "Triple Negative" = "black", "Not Triple Negative" = "#ff0000")         ),     options = ggoncoplot_options(       show_genebar_labels = TRUE,        plotsize_metadata_rel_height = 40,        plotsize_tmb_rel_height = 10,        genebar_label_nudge = 20,        fontsize_genes = 11,       fontsize_metadata_text = 11,       show_legend = FALSE     ),     interactive = FALSE,     verbose=FALSE   )   ## Create a gene expression t-SNE describing the same BRCA cohort tsne_expression <- express_precomputed("BRCA", datatype = "expression", interactive = FALSE)  ## Create a methylation UMAP describing the same BRCA cohort umap_methylation <- express_precomputed("BRCA", datatype = "methylation", interactive = FALSE)  ## Combine plots with patchwork combined_plots <- (tsne_expression + umap_methylation) / ggoncoplot  + plot_layout(heights = c(3.5, 6.5))  # View the interactive version with ggiraph interactive_multiplot <- girafe(ggobj = combined_plots, height_svg = 6, width_svg = 9)  # Add some settings to choose how to make combined plots interactive interactive_multiplot <- girafe_options(x = interactive_multiplot,     opts_selection(type = "multiple", only_shiny = FALSE, css = "opacity: 1"),     opts_selection_inv(css = "opacity: 0.12")     )  interactive_multiplot

title: Manual
text: current version ggoncoplot, download button interactive plot downloads low-resolution image. recommend following alternatives high-quality plots suitable scientific publications: save non-interactive plot 300 dpi PNG, PDF, SVG, use ggsaves function ggsaves. save interactive plot HTML, SVG, PDF formats (high-resolution PNGs can derived), use ggisaves function ggsaves. Alternatives: - Export non-interactive graph format using preferred export method R plots (e.g., RStudio GUI, ggplot2::ggsave()). - Export interactive graph format using preferred export method R HTML widgets (e.g., RStudio GUI htmlwidgets::saveWidget()).

title: Sorting Algorithm
text: common approach sorting oncoplot hierarchical sort, follows steps:

what: Step 1: Identify Sort Order for Y Axis
title: Sorting Algorithm
text: Determine genes mutated greatest proportion samples (multi-hits gene don’t count towards quantification recurrence). Identify top N genes (default 10) based recurrent mutations across samples. Rank genes based degree recurrence. ranking determines sort order Y axis.

what: Step 2: Identify Sort Order for X Axis
title: Sorting Algorithm
text: X axis sorted based Y axis.

previous_headings: How are oncoplot axes sorted? > Step 2: Identify Sort Order for X Axis
title: Sorting Algorithm
text: Rank sample samples mutations recurrently mutated gene appear furthest left oncoplot. followed samples mutations second recurrently mutated gene, . Within group samples mutations specific gene, want sort based whether next mutated gene mutated, continue process hierarchically.

previous_headings: How are oncoplot axes sorted? > Step 2: Identify Sort Order for X Axis
title: Sorting Algorithm
text: X axis hierarchical sort achieved using base-2 scoring system. display top 5 recurrently mutated genes Y axis, allocate weighting gene based ranking order recurrence: recurrent gene weight 2(5−1)=162^{(5-1)} = 16. second recurrent gene weight 2(4−1)=82^{(4-1)} = 8. . sample’s X axis sorting rank calculated summing weights genes mutated sample.

code: # Install ggEDA (dependency) install.packages('ggEDA', repos = c('https://ccicb.r-universe.dev'))  # Install ggOncoplot install.packages('ggoncoplot', repos = c('https://selkamand.r-universe.dev', 'https://cloud.r-project.org')) # Install remotes package (required to install from github) if (!requireNamespace("remotes", quietly = TRUE))    install.packages("remotes")  # Install ggoncoplot remotes::install_github('selkamand/ggoncoplot', build_vignettes = TRUE)

title: Easily Create Interactive Oncoplots
text: Oncoplots highly effective visualising mutation data cancer cohorts challenging generate major R plotting systems (base, lattice, ggplot2) due algorithmic graphical complexity. Simplifying process generating oncoplots make accessible researchers. Existing packages including ComplexHeatmap, maftools, genVisR make static oncoplots easier create, still significant unmet need user-friendly method creating oncoplots following features: Interactive plots: Customizable tooltips, cross-selection samples across different plots, auto-copying sample identifiers click. enables exploration multiomic datasets. Support tidy datasets: Compatibility tidy, tabular mutation-level formats cancer cohort datasets typically stored . greatly improves range datasets can quickly easily visualised oncoplot since genomic data Mutation Annotation Format (MAF) files relational databases usually follow structure. Auto-colouring: Automatic selection accessible colour palettes datasets consequence annotations aligned standard variant effect dictionaries including Prediction Annotation Variant Effects (PAVE), Sequence Ontology () MAF Variant Classifications. Versatility: ability visualize entities gene mutations, noncoding features (e.g., promoter enhancer mutations) non-genomic entities (e.g., microbial presence microbiome datasets). developed ggoncoplot first R package address challenges together. Examples key features available ggoncoplot manual. full comparison ggoncoplot features similar tools available

title: Easily Create Interactive Oncoplots
text: ggOncoplot can produce default, interactive oncoplot even largest TCGA cancer cohort (Breast Cancer - BRCA) contains 1026 samples 0.81 seconds (Macbook Pro; M3 Pro Chip; 18GB ram). Since number mutations genomic dataset change number tiles rendered final oncoplot, 10x increase variant number (1,350,300) takes 1.3x longer plot. Code used benchmarking shown .
code: library(microbenchmark) # install.packages("microbenchmark")  # Setup Data data <- read.csv(system.file("testdata/BRCA_tcgamutations_mc3.csv.gz", package = "ggoncoplot"))  # Increase variant count by 10x data_10x <- do.call("rbind", replicate(n = 10, data, simplify = FALSE))  # Benchmark microbenchmark(   interactive = print(ggoncoplot(data, col_samples = "Sample", col_genes = "Gene", col_mutation_type = "MutationType", verbose = FALSE)),   interactive_10x = print(ggoncoplot(data_10x, col_samples = "Sample", col_genes = "Gene", col_mutation_type = "MutationType", verbose = FALSE)),   static = print(ggoncoplot(data, col_samples = "Sample", col_genes = "Gene", col_mutation_type = "MutationType", verbose = FALSE, interactive = FALSE)),   static_10x = print(ggoncoplot(data_10x, col_samples = "Sample", col_genes = "Gene", col_mutation_type = "MutationType", verbose = FALSE, interactive = FALSE)),   times = 18 )

title: Easily Create Interactive Oncoplots
text: Responsiveness interactive graphics may slow number tiles oncoplot (samples x genes increases) especially tooltips contain large amounts information. MacBook Pro (M3 Pro chip 18GB memory) largest TCGA dataset (BRCA) including 1026 samples can rendered sample IDs tooltip noticeable delay tooltip responsiveness.

title: ggoncoplot — ggoncoplot
text: Creates interactive oncoplot visualize mutation landscape cancer cohorts.

title: ggoncoplot — ggoncoplot
code: ggoncoplot(   data,   col_genes,   col_samples,   col_mutation_type = NULL,   genes_to_include = NULL,   genes_to_ignore = NULL,   col_tooltip = col_samples,   topn = 10,   return_extra_genes_if_tied = FALSE,   draw_gene_barplot = FALSE,   draw_tmb_barplot = FALSE,   copy = c("sample", "gene", "tooltip", "mutation_type", "nothing"),   palette = NULL,   metadata = NULL,   metadata_palette = NULL,   col_samples_metadata = col_samples,   cols_to_plot_metadata = NULL,   metadata_require_mutations = TRUE,   pathway = NULL,   col_genes_pathway = col_genes,   show_all_samples = FALSE,   total_samples = c("any_mutations", "all", "oncoplot"),   sample_order = NULL,   metadata_sort_cols = NULL,   metadata_sort_desc = TRUE,   metadata_sort_by = "frequency",   tmb_data = NULL,   tmb_palette = NULL,   interactive = TRUE,   options = ggoncoplot_options(),   verbose = TRUE )

title: ggoncoplot — ggoncoplot
text: data data oncoplot. data.frame 1 row per mutation cohort. Must contain columns describing gene_symbols sample_identifiers (data.frame) col_genes name data column containing gene names/symbols (string) col_samples name data column containing sample identifiers (string) col_mutation_type name data column describing mutation types (string, optional) genes_to_include specific genes include oncoplot (character, optional) genes_to_ignore names genes ignored (character, optional) col_tooltip name data column containing whatever information want display (string, defaults col_samples) topn many top genes visualize. Ignored genes_to_include supplied (number, default 10) return_extra_genes_if_tied instead strictly returning topn genes, case ties (multiple genes mutated exact number samples, complicating selection top n genes), return tied genes (potentially topn). FALSE, return strictly topn genes, breaking ties based order appearance dataset (flag, default FALSE) draw_gene_barplot add barplot describing number samples gene mutated (right side) (flag, default FALSE) draw_tmb_barplot add barplot describing total number mutations sample (main plot). single gene mutated multiple times, mutations counted towards total (flag, default FALSE) copy value copy clipboard oncoplot tile clicked (string, one 'sample', 'gene', 'tooltip', 'mutation_type', 'nothing', default 'sample') palette named vector mapping possible mutation types (vector names) colors (vector values, optional) metadata dataframe describing sample level metadata. One column must contain unique sample identifiers. columns can describe numeric / categorical metadata (data.frame, optional) metadata_palette list named vectors. List names correspond metadata column names (categorical ). Vector names levels columns. Vector values colors, vector names used map values data color. (optional) col_samples_metadata column metadata data.frame describes sample identifiers (string, defaults col_samples) cols_to_plot_metadata names columns metadata plotted (character, optional) metadata_require_mutations filter samples metadata lacking mutations data (flag, default TRUE) pathway two column dataframe describing pathway. column containing gene names name col_gene (data.frame, optional) col_genes_pathway column pathway data.frame describes gene names (string, defaults col_genes) show_all_samples show samples oncoplot, even mutations selected genes. Samples described metadata mutations still filtered default, can show setting metadata_require_mutations = FALSE (flag, default FALSE) total_samples Strategy calculating total number samples. value used compute proportion mutation recurrence displayed tooltip hovering gene barplot, text annotation ggoncoplot_options(show_genebar_labels = TRUE) set TRUE. Possible values: any_mutations: samples data (mutation dataset), irrespective whether oncoplot . oncoplot: samples present oncoplot. : samples either data metadata. sample_order sample IDs order shown oncoplot (left right). Overrides gene-based auto-ranking. (character vector). metadata_sort_cols character vector metadata columns sort . NULL default typical gene-based sort unless sample_order specified. metadata_sort_desc Logical scalar vector indicating whether rank column descending order. single value supplied recycled columns. metadata_sort_by Character vector specifying rank non-numeric column. Valid values include "alphabetical" "frequency". single value supplied recycled columns. numeric columns, sort_by ignored ranking always based numeric order. tmb_data Optional custom TMB dataset. data.frame 2–3 columns including col_samples. Column mapping inferred follows: sample column: column named col_samples TMB column: first numeric non-sample column subtype column (optional): third column present, treated stacking/colouring subtype missing values permitted. Note: stacked bars disabled log10_transform_tmb = TRUE (totals shown). tmb_palette named vector mapping possible tmb sub types (vector names) colors (vector values). tmb_palette tmb_data NULL, set match palette. interactive plot interactive (boolean, default TRUE) options list additional visual parameters created calling ggoncoplot_options(). See ggoncoplot_options details. verbose verbose mode (flag, default TRUE)

Set ylab_title = NULL remove title (string) sample_id_position sample names x axis top bottom main oncoplot (string) sample_id_angle angle sample names (number) fontsize_xlab size x axis title (number) fontsize_ylab size y axis title (number) fontsize_genes size y axis text (gene names) (number) fontsize_samples size x axis text (sample names). Ignored unless show_sample_ids set true (number) fontsize_count fontsize gene mutation count x axis (number) fontsize_tmb_title fontsize y axis title TMB marginal plot (number) fontsize_tmb_axis fontsize y axis text TMB marginal plot (number) fontsize_pathway fontsize y axis strip text describing gene pathways (number) fontsize_legend_title fontsize legend titles  (number) fontsize_legend_text fontsize legend text (number) fontface_genes font face gene names. One ("plain", "italic", "bold", "bold.italic"). fontface_samples font face sample names. One ("plain", "italic", "bold", "bold.italic"). fontface_metadata_text font face metadata columns. One ("plain", "italic", "bold", "bold.italic"). tile_height proportion available vertical space tile take (0-1) (number) tile_width proportion available horizontal space tile take (0-1) (number) colour_backround colour used background non-mutated tiles (string) colour_mutation_type_unspecified colour mutations oncoplot margin plots col_mutation_type supplied (string) show_sample_ids show sample_ids_on_x_axis (flag) show_ylab_title show y axis title oncoplot (flag) show_xlab_title show x axis title oncoplot (flag) show_ylab_title_tmb show y axis title TMB margin plot (flag) show_legend show oncoplot legend show_legend_titles show legend titles (flag) show_axis_gene show x axis line/ticks/labels gene barplot (flag) show_genebar_labels gene barplot labelled % samples gene mutated (flag) show_axis_tmb show y axis line/ticks/labels TMB barplot (flag) log10_transform_tmb log10 transform total number mutations TMB marginal plot (flag) scientific_tmb display tmb counts scientific notation (flag) genebar_label_padding much padding add x axis gene barplot (number) genebar_only_pad_when_labels_shown apply genebar_label_padding labels shown (flag) genebar_label_nudge much padding add gene barplot bar annotations (number) genebar_label_round many digits round genebar labels (number) genebar_scale_breaks fine-grained control x axis breaks gene barplot. One : NULL minor breaks waiver() default breaks (none discrete, one minor break major break continuous) numeric vector positions function given limits returns vector minor breaks. function two arguments, given limits major break positions. genebar_scale_n_breaks integer guiding number breaks algorithm may choose slightly different number ensure nice break labels. effect genebar_scale_breaks = ggplot2::waiver(). Use NULL use default colour_pathway_text colour text describing pathways (string) colour_pathway_bg background fill colour pathway strips (string) colour_pathway_outline outline colour pathway strips (string) pathway_text_angle angle pathway text label (typically 0 90 degrees) (number) ggoncoplot_guide_ncol many columns use describing oncoplot legend (number) legend_key_size width legend key block (number) prettify_legend_titles legend titles prettified human-readable forms (e.g. converting 'my_title' 'Title'). Prettification can customised using 'prettify_function' argument (flag) prettify_legend_values legend values prettified human-readable forms (e.g. converting 'my_title' 'Title'). Prettification can customised using 'prettify_function' argument (flag) prettify_function function takes string returns nicely formatted string. Used prettify legend titles values  (function) metadata_position metadata plot top bottom oncoplot. fontsize_metadata_text fontsize y axis text sample metadata plot (number) fontsize_metadata_legend_title fontsize titles metadata legends. default fontsize_legend_text (number) fontsize_metadata_legend_text fontsize text metadata legends. default fontsize_legend_title (number) fontsize_metadata_barplot_y_numbers fontsize text describing numeric barplot max & min values (number) metadata_legend_nrow number rows allowed per metadata legend (number) metadata_legend_ncol number columns allowed per metadata legend (number) metadata_legend_key_size width legend key block (number). Defaults legend_key_size metadata_na_marker character used indicate data missing (string) metadata_na_marker_size size character used data missing (number) metadata_maxlevels categorical variables, maximum number distinct values allow (many make hard find palette suits) (number) metadata_numeric_plot_type visual representation numeric properties. One 'bar', bar charts, 'heatmap' heatmaps metadata_legend_orientation_heatmap orientation heatmaps legends. One "horizontal" "vertical" number breaks given transformation. metadata_colours_default Default colors categorical variables without custom palette.

title: ggoncoplot options — ggoncoplot_options
text: ggoncoplot options object ready passed ggoncoplot() options argument

title: ggoncoplot options — ggoncoplot_options
code:
# Read GBM MAF file gbm_csv <- system.file(   package = "ggoncoplot",   "testdata/GBM_tcgamutations_mc3_maf.csv.gz" ) gbm_df <- read.csv(file = gbm_csv, header = TRUE)  # Plot Oncoplot and Customise Options gbm_df |>   ggoncoplot(     col_genes = "Hugo_Symbol",     col_samples = "Tumor_Sample_Barcode",     col_mutation_type = "Variant_Classification",      # Customise Visual Options     options = ggoncoplot_options(        # Interactive Plot Options       interactive_svg_width = 12,       interactive_svg_height = 6,        # Relative height of different plotsizes       plotsize_tmb_rel_height = 10,       plotsize_gene_rel_width = 20,       plotsize_metadata_rel_height = 20,        # Axis Titles       xlab_title = "Glioblastoma Samples",       ylab_title = "Top 10 mutated genes",        # Fontsizes       fontsize_xlab = 40,       fontsize_ylab = 40,       fontsize_genes = 16,       fontsize_samples = 12,       fontsize_count = 14,       fontsize_tmb_title = 14,       fontsize_tmb_axis = 11,       fontsize_pathway = 16,        # Customise Tiles       tile_height = 1,       tile_width = 1,       colour_backround = "grey90",       colour_mutation_type_unspecified = "grey10",        # Show different elements       show_sample_ids = FALSE,       show_ylab_title = FALSE,       show_xlab_title = FALSE,       show_ylab_title_tmb = FALSE,       show_axis_gene = TRUE,       show_axis_tmb = TRUE,        # Transformation and label scales       log10_transform_tmb = TRUE,       scientific_tmb = FALSE,        # Gene Barplot Specific Options       show_genebar_labels = TRUE,       genebar_label_padding = 0.2,       genebar_only_pad_when_labels_shown = TRUE,       genebar_label_nudge = 2,       genebar_label_round = 1,        # Pathway Faceting Colours / Text       colour_pathway_text = "white",       colour_pathway_bg = "grey10",       colour_pathway_outline = "black",       pathway_text_angle = 0,        # Legend number of columns       ggoncoplot_guide_ncol = 2     )   ) #>  #> ── Identify Class ── #>  #> ℹ Found 9 unique mutation types in input set #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid PAVE terms #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid SO terms #> ℹ 9/9 mutation types were valid MAF terms #> ✔ Mutation Types are described using valid MAF terms ... using MAF palete  {"x":{"html":"<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

title: Plot oncoplot — ggoncoplot_plot
text: function takes output ggoncoplot_prep_df plots . exposed since makes assumptions structure input data.

title: Plot oncoplot — ggoncoplot_plot
code: ggoncoplot_plot(   data,   show_sample_ids = FALSE,   palette = NULL,   show_ylab_title = FALSE,   show_xlab_title = FALSE,   xlab_title = "Sample",   ylab_title = "Gene",   sample_id_position = c("bottom", "top"),   sample_id_angle = 90,   fontsize_xlab = 16,   fontsize_ylab = 16,   fontsize_genes = 14,   fontsize_samples = 10,   fontface_genes = "plain",   fontface_samples = "plain",   fontsize_legend_title = 12,   fontsize_legend_text = 12,   tile_height = 1,   tile_width = 1,   copy = c("sample", "gene", "tooltip", "mutation_type", "nothing"),   colour_backround = "grey90",   colour_mutation_type_unspecified = "grey10",   fontsize_pathway = 16,   colour_pathway_text = "white",   colour_pathway_bg = "grey10",   colour_pathway_outline = "black",   pathway_text_angle = 0,   legend_title = "Mutation Type",   show_legend_titles = TRUE,   ggoncoplot_guide_ncol = 2,   legend_key_size = 0.3,   margin_t = 0.2,   margin_r = 0.3,   margin_b = 0.2,   margin_l = 0.3,   margin_unit = "cm",   mutation_type_supplied = TRUE,   prettify_legend_values = TRUE,   prettify_function = prettify )

title: Plot oncoplot — ggoncoplot_plot
text: data transformed data ggoncoplot_prep_df() (data.frame) show_sample_ids show sample_ids_on_x_axis (flag) palette named vector mapping possible mutation types (vector names) colors (vector values, optional) show_ylab_title show y axis title oncoplot (flag) show_xlab_title show x axis title oncoplot (flag) xlab_title x axis label. Set xlab_title = NULL remove title (string) ylab_title y axis interactive plot. Set ylab_title = NULL remove title (string) sample_id_position sample names x axis top bottom main oncoplot (string) sample_id_angle angle sample names (number) fontsize_xlab size x axis title (number) fontsize_ylab size y axis title (number) fontsize_genes size y axis text (gene names) (number) fontsize_samples size x axis text (sample names). Ignored unless show_sample_ids set true (number) fontface_genes font face gene names. One ("plain", "italic", "bold", "bold.italic"). fontface_samples font face sample names. One ("plain", "italic", "bold", "bold.italic"). fontsize_legend_title fontsize legend titles  (number) fontsize_legend_text fontsize legend text (number) tile_height proportion available vertical space tile take (0-1) (number) tile_width proportion available horizontal space tile take (0-1) (number) copy value copy clipboard oncoplot tile clicked (string, one 'sample', 'gene', 'tooltip', 'mutation_type', 'nothing', default 'sample') colour_backround colour used background non-mutated tiles (string) colour_mutation_type_unspecified colour mutations oncoplot margin plots col_mutation_type supplied (string) fontsize_pathway fontsize y axis strip text describing gene pathways (number) colour_pathway_text colour text describing pathways (string) colour_pathway_bg background fill colour pathway strips (string) colour_pathway_outline outline colour pathway strips (string) pathway_text_angle angle pathway text label (typically 0 90 degrees) (number) legend_title name legend title (string) show_legend_titles show legend titles (flag) ggoncoplot_guide_ncol many columns use describing oncoplot legend (number) legend_key_size width legend key block (number) margin_t, margin_r, margin_b, margin_l margin top, right, bottom, left side plot. default, unit 'cm' can changed setting margin_unit value ggplot2::margin() understand (number) margin_unit Unit margin specification. default 'cm' can changed setting margin_unit value ggplot2::margin() understand (string) mutation_type_supplied user supply mutation_type column? , turn legend. prettify_legend_values legend values prettified human-readable forms (e.g. converting 'my_title' 'Title'). Prettification can customised using 'prettify_function' argument (flag) prettify_function function takes string returns nicely formatted string. Used prettify legend titles values  (function)

title: Plot oncoplot — ggoncoplot_plot
text: ggplot girafe object interactive=TRUE

title: Plot oncoplot — ggoncoplot_plot
code:
# ===== GBM ===== gbm_csv <- system.file(   package = "ggoncoplot",   "testdata/GBM_tcgamutations_mc3_maf.csv.gz" )  gbm_clinical_csv <- system.file(   package = "ggoncoplot",   "testdata/GBM_tcgamutations_mc3_clinical.csv" )  gbm_df <- read.csv(file = gbm_csv, header = TRUE) gbm_clinical_df <- read.csv(file = gbm_clinical_csv, header = TRUE)  # Plot Basic Oncoplot ggoncoplot(   gbm_df,   "Hugo_Symbol",   "Tumor_Sample_Barcode",   col_mutation_type = "Variant_Classification",   metadata = gbm_clinical_df,   cols_to_plot_metadata = "gender" ) #> ℹ 2 samples with metadata have no mutations. Fitering these out #> ℹ To keep these samples, set `metadata_require_mutations = FALSE`. To view them in the oncoplot ensure you additionally set `show_all_samples = TRUE` #> → TCGA-06-0165-01 #> → TCGA-06-0167-01 #>  #> ── Identify Class ── #>  #> ℹ Found 9 unique mutation types in input set #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid PAVE terms #> ℹ 0/9 mutation types were valid SO terms #> ℹ 9/9 mutation types were valid MAF terms #> ✔ Mutation Types are described using valid MAF terms ... using MAF palete #>  #> ── Plotting Sample Metadata ──────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ── Sorting  #> ℹ Sorting X axis by: Order of appearance #>  #> ── Generating Plot  #> ℹ Found 1 plottable columns in data  {"x":{"html":"<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

title: Prep data for oncoplot — ggoncoplot_prep_df

title: Prep data for oncoplot — ggoncoplot_prep_df
code: ggoncoplot_prep_df(   data,   col_genes,   col_samples,   genes_for_oncoplot,   col_mutation_type = NULL,   col_tooltip = col_samples,   pathway = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Prep data for oncoplot — ggoncoplot_prep_df
text: data data oncoplot. data.frame 1 row per mutation cohort. Must contain columns describing gene_symbols sample_identifiers (data.frame) col_genes name data column containing gene names/symbols (string) col_samples name data column containing sample identifiers (string) genes_for_oncoplot list genes include oncoplot (character). col_mutation_type name data column describing mutation types (string) col_tooltip name data column containing whatever information want display (string) pathway two column dataframe describing pathway. column containing gene names name col_gene (data.frame, optional) verbose verbose mode (flag, default TRUE)

title: Prep data for oncoplot — ggoncoplot_prep_df
text: dataframe following columns: 'Gene', 'Sample', 'MutationType', 'Tooltip'. Sample factor levels sorted appropriate order oncoplot vis. Genes represents either topn genes specific genes set genes_to_include

title: Prep data for oncoplot — ggoncoplot_prep_df
code: #' # ===== GBM ===== gbm_csv <- system.file(   package = "ggoncoplot",   "testdata/GBM_tcgamutations_mc3_maf.csv.gz" )  gbm_df <- read.csv(file = gbm_csv, header = TRUE)  # Get genes in appropriate order for oncoplot genes_for_oncoplot <- ggoncoplot:::get_genes_for_oncoplot(   data = gbm_df,   col_samples = "Tumor_Sample_Barcode",   col_genes = "Hugo_Symbol",   topn = 20,   verbose = FALSE )  # Create dataframe basis of oncoplot (1 row per sample-gene combo) ggoncoplot:::ggoncoplot_prep_df(   gbm_df,   col_genes = "Hugo_Symbol",   col_samples = "Tumor_Sample_Barcode",   col_mutation_type = "Variant_Classification",   genes_for_oncoplot = genes_for_oncoplot ) #> # A tibble: 1,057 × 5 #>    Sample          Gene   MutationType      MutationCount Tooltip         #>    <fct>           <fct>  <chr>                     <int> <chr>           #>  1 TCGA-19-5956-01 PTEN   Multi_Hit                     3 TCGA-19-5956-01 #>  2 TCGA-19-5956-01 TP53   Multi_Hit                     2 TCGA-19-5956-01 #>  3 TCGA-19-5956-01 TTN    Multi_Hit                    28 TCGA-19-5956-01 #>  4 TCGA-19-5956-01 EGFR   Missense_Mutation             1 TCGA-19-5956-01 #>  5 TCGA-19-5956-01 MUC16  Multi_Hit                    15 TCGA-19-5956-01 #>  6 TCGA-19-5956-01 FLG    Multi_Hit                     8 TCGA-19-5956-01 #>  7 TCGA-19-5956-01 NF1    Multi_Hit                     3 TCGA-19-5956-01 #>  8 TCGA-19-5956-01 RYR2   Missense_Mutation             1 TCGA-19-5956-01 #>  9 TCGA-19-5956-01 ATRX   Multi_Hit                     5 TCGA-19-5956-01 #> 10 TCGA-19-5956-01 PIK3R1 Multi_Hit                     2 TCGA-19-5956-01 #> # ℹ 1,047 more rows

what: Identify top genes from a mutation df — identify_topn_genes
title: Identify top genes from a mutation df — identify_topn_genes
text: Identify top genes mutation df

title: Identify top genes from a mutation df — identify_topn_genes
code: identify_topn_genes(   data,   col_samples,   col_genes,   topn,   genes_to_ignore = NULL,   return_extra_genes_if_tied = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Identify top genes from a mutation df — identify_topn_genes
text: data data oncoplot. data.frame 1 row per mutation cohort. Must contain columns describing gene_symbols sample_identifiers (data.frame) col_samples name data column containing sample identifiers (string) col_genes name data column containing gene names/symbols (string) topn many top genes visualize. Ignored genes_to_include supplied (number, default 10) genes_to_ignore names genes ignored (character, optional) return_extra_genes_if_tied instead strictly returning topn genes, case ties (multiple genes mutated exact number samples, complicating selection top n genes), return tied genes (potentially topn). FALSE, return strictly topn genes, breaking ties based order appearance dataset (flag, default FALSE) verbose verbose mode (flag, default TRUE)

title: Identify top genes from a mutation df — identify_topn_genes
text: vector topn genes. order rank (mutated = first) (character)

title: Simulated Cancer Dataset Metadata — oncosim_metadata
text: data frame 11 rows 6 columns: Samples Unique sample identifiers numeric_feature Numeric variable including zeros, positive negative values, NA, Inf/-Inf categorical_feature4levels Categorical variable four levels ("cat", "dog", "magpie", "giraffe"), may contain empty strings NA clinical_feature2levels Clinical categorical variable indicating biological sex two levels ("male", "female"), may contain NA logical_feature Logical variable TRUE, FALSE, NA numeric_that_could_be_logical Integer variable coded 0 1 (NA) interpreted logical

title: Simulated Cancer Dataset Metadata — oncosim_metadata

title: Make strings prettier for printing — prettify
text: string input string space_after_apostrophe add space apostrophe long alphanumeric character followed anything space (flag) autodetect_units automatically detect units (e.g. mm, kg, etc) wrap brackets.

title: Calculate Pathway-informed Genes Rankings — rank_genes_based_on_pathways
text: genes appear top oncoplot? function takes pathway gene ranks returns list genes sorted first pathway gene rank. Gene & pathway rankings can calculated upstream. default use order gene_pathway_map.

title: Calculate Pathway-informed Genes Rankings — rank_genes_based_on_pathways
code: rank_genes_based_on_pathways(   gene_pathway_map,   generanks = unique(as.character(gene_pathway_map[[1]])),   pathwayranks = unique(as.character(gene_pathway_map[[2]])) )

title: Calculate Pathway-informed Genes Rankings — rank_genes_based_on_pathways
text: gene_pathway_map dataframe column 1 = gene names column 2 = pathway names generanks gene names order ranked, earlier vector = oncoplot. (character) pathwayranks pathway names order ranked, earlier vector = oncoplot (character)

title: Calculate Pathway-informed Genes Rankings — rank_genes_based_on_pathways
text: gene names, sorted based order appear oncoplot (first = top). returns genes present generanks (character)

what: Generate score based on genes — score_based_on_gene_rank
title: Generate score based on genes — score_based_on_gene_rank
text: Score used sort samples based genes mutated. Make sure run one sample (use grouping)

title: Generate score based on genes — score_based_on_gene_rank
code: score_based_on_gene_rank(   mutated_genes,   genes_informing_score,   gene_rank,   debug_mode = FALSE )

title: Generate score based on genes — score_based_on_gene_rank
text: mutated_genes vector genes mutated single sample (character) genes_informing_score genes determine sort order? (character) gene_rank order importance genes used determine sort order. Higher number = higher sort order (character) debug_mode debug mode (flag)

title: Generate score based on genes — score_based_on_gene_rank
text: score (higher = higher sorting order) (number)

title: Generate score based on genes — score_based_on_gene_rank
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # First set of genes has a high rank since both BRCA2 and EGFR are mutated score_based_on_gene_rank(c("TERT", "EGFR", "PTEN", "BRCA2"), c("EGFR", "BRCA2"), gene_rank = 1:2)  # If EGFR is mutated without BRCA2, we get a lower score score_based_on_gene_rank(c("TERT", "EGFR", "PTEN", "IDH1"), c("EGFR", "BRCA2"), gene_rank = 1:2)  # If BRCA2 is mutated without EGFR, # we get a score lower than BRCA2+EGFR but higher than EGFR alone due to higher gene_rank of BRCA2 score_based_on_gene_rank(c("TERT", "IDH1", "PTEN", "BRCA2"), c("EGFR", "BRCA2"), gene_rank = 1:2) } # }
